page: Amazon Web Services g4ad.2xlarge is a graphics-focused instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, corresponding to 4 physical cores and 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For parallel computing and graphics workloads, the instance includes a single AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 300 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is rated at a 4 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations show mixed performance characteristics. The instance exhibits weak CPU performance in stress-ng tests, with single-core results in the bottom 10% and multi-core results in the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds are also weak, falling into the bottom 25%. However, the instance achieves average scores in Geekbench single-core, multi-core, and ray tracing tests, as well as average performance in Passmark CPU, memory, and database operations. Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput also fall within the average range. For machine learning, LLM inference speeds are average for small 135M models but drop to weak for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the g4ad.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring both local NVMe storage and GPU acceleration without the premium of high-end enterprise GPUs. The primary tradeoff lies in its lower raw CPU compute and large-block memory bandwidth compared to compute-optimized instances. This profile makes it suitable for graphics-intensive applications, moderate database operations, software compilation, and light machine learning inference rather than heavy CPU-bound processing or large-scale LLM deployments.
description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.2xlarge is a graphics-focused instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs, 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a single AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. It includes 300 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average performance in Geekbench, Passmark CPU, and database operations, though raw CPU stress tests and large-block memory bandwidth fall into the lower percentiles. The instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads that require a balance of local NVMe storage and hardware-accelerated graphics without the expense of high-end compute platforms. It is suitable for graphics-intensive applications, software compilation, and moderate database operations.

tagline: An AMD-powered instance featuring dedicated GPU acceleration, local NVMe storage, and balanced memory for graphics-intensive and database workloads.

Dedicated AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPU with 8 GB VRAM
300 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
